general.languageDescription: Select your preferred language for the interface.
general.themeDescription: Choose the appearance of the interface.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.description: Configure how long audit logs and activity events are kept. Set to 0 to keep forever.
retention.auditDaysDescription: Number of days to keep audit log entries. Older entries are automatically purged.
retention.activityDaysDescription: Number of days to keep container activity events. Older events are automatically purged.

auth.description: Configure authentication providers and session settings.
auth.localAuthDescription: Username and password authentication is enabled by default.
auth.oidcLdapDescription: External authentication providers are not yet configured.

https.subject: Subject:
https.enableHttpsDescription: Serve traffic over TLS using the uploaded certificate.
https.forceHttpsDescription: Redirect HTTP to HTTPS via X-Forwarded-Proto. For reverse proxy setups.
https.restartDescription: TLS changes take effect after container restart.

agent.description: Configure how McHarbor communicates with remote agents. These settings affect event collection, heartbeat pings, metrics, and request timeouts for all agent-connected environments.
agent.eventModeDescription: How Docker events are collected from agent environments.
agent.streamWarning: Streaming events monopolizes the agent WebSocket connection, which can block other requests like container inspect and stats. Only use this if you need real-time events and have few containers.
agent.eventPollIntervalDescription: How often to fetch new events from agent environments. Lower values mean more frequent updates but higher load.
agent.pingIntervalDescription: How often to send keepalive pings to connected agents.
agent.metricsEnabledDescription: Enable host metrics collection for agent environments. One-shot stats take ~2s per container through the agent transport.
agent.requestTimeoutDescription: Maximum time to wait for a response from the agent before timing out.

webhooks.description: Configure webhook endpoints for event notifications.
webhooks.confirmDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete this webhook.
webhooks.dialogDescription: Send HTTP POST requests when events occur.

plugins.dialogDescription: Add a new plugin from a URL or local path.

schedules.description: Configure scheduled tasks like container operations and maintenance.
schedules.confirmDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete this schedule.

email.description: Configure email servers for sending notifications, alerts, and reports.
email.selectTypeDescription: Choose the type of email server you want to configure.
email.typeSMTPDescription: Connect to any SMTP server with PLAIN, LOGIN, or CRAM-MD5 authentication.
email.typeExchangeDescription: Send emails via Microsoft Graph API using OAuth2 client credentials.
email.typeGmailDescription: Send emails via Gmail API using OAuth2 client credentials.
email.confirmDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete this email server configuration.
email.testDialogDescription: Send a test email via {{name}} to verify the configuration.

exchange.title: Exchange Online Setup Guide
exchange.step1Title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
exchange.step1Description: Go to Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > App registrations > New registration.
exchange.step2Description: Add Microsoft Graph > Application permissions > Mail.Send. Grant admin consent.
exchange.step3Description: Go to Certificates & secrets > New client secret. Copy the value immediately.
exchange.step4Description: Copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID from the Overview page.
exchange.step5Description: The from address must be a valid mailbox in your Microsoft 365 tenant.

gmail.title: Gmail Setup Guide
gmail.step1Description: Go to Google Cloud Console > Create a new project or select an existing one.
gmail.step2Description: Go to APIs & Services > Library > Search for Gmail API > Enable.
gmail.step3Description: Go to APIs & Services > Credentials > Create credentials > OAuth client ID. Select 'Web application'.
gmail.step4Description: For service accounts, enable domain-wide delegation and authorize the Gmail send scope.

communications.description: Configure notification channels for alerts, events, and system messages.
communications.selectTypeDescription: Choose the notification platform you want to configure.
communications.typeSlackDescription: Send notifications to a Slack channel via incoming webhook.
communications.typeDiscordDescription: Send notifications to a Discord channel via webhook.
communications.typeTeamsDescription: Send Adaptive Card notifications to Microsoft Teams.
communications.typeGotifyDescription: Push notifications to a self-hosted Gotify server.
communications.typeNtfyDescription: Send push notifications via ntfy (self-hosted or ntfy.sh).
communications.typeTelegramDescription: Send messages to a Telegram chat via Bot API.
communications.typeSignalDescription: Send messages via a Signal REST API server.
communications.typeWhatsappDescription: Send messages via the WhatsApp Cloud API.
communications.confirmDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete this notification channel configuration.
communications.testDialogDescription: Send a test notification via {{name}} to verify the configuration.

teams.title: Microsoft Teams Setup Guide
teams.step1Description: Go to the channel where you want notifications and click the three-dot menu.
teams.step2Description: Select Workflows > Post to a channel when a webhook request is received.
teams.step3Description: Give it a name and select the channel. The workflow will be created automatically.
teams.step4Description: Copy the webhook URL provided by the workflow and paste it above.

gotify.title: Gotify Setup Guide
gotify.step1Description: Deploy Gotify via Docker: docker run -p 80:80 gotify/server
gotify.step2Description: Open the Gotify web UI, go to Apps, and create a new application.
gotify.step3Description: Copy the token shown for your new application.
gotify.step4Description: Enter your Gotify server URL and app token above.

telegram.title: Telegram Bot Setup Guide
telegram.step1Description: Message @BotFather on Telegram and send /newbot. Follow the prompts to create your bot.
telegram.step2Description: BotFather will give you an API token. Copy it and paste it above.
telegram.step3Description: Add the bot to your group/channel. Send a message, then visit https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates to find the chat ID.
telegram.step4Description: Enter the bot token and chat ID above.

signal.title: Signal REST API Setup Guide
signal.step1Description: Run the Signal REST API server via Docker: docker run -p 8080:8080 bbernhard/signal-cli-rest-api
signal.step2Description: Register your Signal phone number with the REST API using the /v1/register endpoint.
signal.step3Description: Verify your phone number using the verification code received via SMS.
signal.step4Description: Enter the server URL, sender number, and recipient numbers above.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp Cloud API Setup Guide
whatsapp.step1Description: Go to developers.facebook.com and create a developer account if you don't have one.
whatsapp.step2Description: Create a new app and select WhatsApp as the product. Set up the WhatsApp Business API.
whatsapp.step3Description: In the WhatsApp dashboard, find your Phone Number ID under the WhatsApp > Getting Started section.
whatsapp.step4Description: Generate a permanent access token in the App Dashboard > WhatsApp > Configuration.
whatsapp.step5Description: Enter the Phone Number ID, access token, and recipient phone number above.

whatsappGateway.title: Self-hosted WhatsApp Gateway Setup Guide
whatsappGateway.step1Description: Set up a self-hosted WhatsApp gateway server (e.g., whatsapp-web.js, Baileys, or similar).
whatsappGateway.step2Description: Ensure your gateway exposes a REST API that accepts POST requests with JSON body.
whatsappGateway.step3Description: Create an authentication token in your gateway's configuration.
whatsappGateway.step4Description: Enter the gateway URL, API token, and recipient phone number above.

whatsappBusiness.title: WhatsApp Business Bot Platform Setup Guide
whatsappBusiness.step1Description: Select a WhatsApp Business API platform provider (e.g., 360dialog, MessageBird, Twilio).
whatsappBusiness.step2Description: Complete the business verification process and set up your WhatsApp Business account.
whatsappBusiness.step3Description: Obtain the API URL, Phone Number ID, and access token from your provider's dashboard.
whatsappBusiness.step4Description: Enter the platform API URL, Phone Number ID, access token, and recipient phone number above.

whatsappSaas.title: Third-party SaaS Setup Guide
whatsappSaas.step1Description: Select a messaging SaaS that supports WhatsApp (e.g., Vonage, Infobip, Gupshup).
whatsappSaas.step2Description: Sign up and configure your WhatsApp messaging channel in the provider's dashboard.
whatsappSaas.step3Description: Obtain the API URL and authentication token from the provider.
whatsappSaas.step4Description: Enter the SaaS API URL, token, and recipient phone number above.

signalBot.title: Signal Bot Setup Guide
signalBot.step1Description: Set up a Signal bot server that exposes a REST API with token authentication.
signalBot.step2Description: Register a phone number or bot identity with the Signal bot server.
signalBot.step3Description: Create an authentication token in the bot server's configuration.
signalBot.step4Description: Enter the bot server URL, API token, and recipient numbers above.

signalD.title: signald Setup Guide
signalD.step1Description: Deploy signald via Docker or from source: docker run -p 15432:15432 signald/signald
signalD.step2Description: Register your Signal phone number with signald using the registration API.
signalD.step3Description: Complete verification with the code received via SMS.
signalD.step4Description: Enter the signald server URL, sender number, and recipient numbers above.

signalSimple.title: Signal Simple Setup Guide
signalSimple.step1Description: Set up a Signal REST API server that does not require authentication.
signalSimple.step2Description: Register and verify your Signal phone number with the server.
signalSimple.step3Description: Enter the server URL, sender number, and recipient numbers above.

alerts.description: Create alert rules with severity, target conditions, and one or more delivery destinations.
alerts.createDescription: Choose what to watch, how to classify it, and where the alert should go.
alerts.editDescription: Update the trigger, target, or delivery destinations for this alert rule.
alerts.noRulesDescription: Create a rule to monitor resource thresholds, container outages, or image updates.
alerts.noChannelsDescription: You can still send alerts to the in-app notification center. Add a communication channel if you also want external delivery.
alerts.channelRequiredHint: Communication channels are optional when in-app notifications are enabled.
alerts.noChannelsInlineHint: No communication channels configured. This rule can still deliver to the notification center.
alerts.inAppDescription: Deliver triggered alerts to the bell inbox in the header.
alerts.destinationRequiredHint: Choose at least one destination before saving.
alerts.enabledDescription: Rule is active and can trigger notifications.
alerts.disabledDescription: Rule is stored but currently muted.

updates.title: Container Auto-Updates
updates.description: Create policies to automatically check for and apply container image updates on a schedule.
updates.createDescription: Define which containers should be automatically updated and when.
updates.containerSelectHint: Select which containers this policy applies to, or choose "All Containers"
updates.strategyHint: How to determine if a newer image is available

about.checkForUpdatesDescription: Check if a newer version of McHarbor is available.
